semanticCore: Retaining, container, vessel, attention, memory, or gathering
quranicRelevance: Retaining ear/mind (Wa'iyah - 'wa ta'iyaha othunon wa'iyah'), storing wealth in containers (Aw'a - 'wa jama'a fa-aw'a'), and containers/bags ('wi'ai akheehi').

keyDerivations.meaning: To retain, comprehend, preserve, keep in memory, or listen attentively
To gather in a vessel, hoard, accumulate, or lock up

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَتَعِيَهَا أُذُنٌ وَاعِيَةٌ (69:12): And that a retaining/conscious ear might retain/preserve it (attentive listening and memory).
وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُوعُونَ (84:23): And God knows best what they hoard/keep inside (their hearts' secrets or plans).
discardedNoise: The physical design of shipping crates.
summaryEn: The root connects memory retention with containers, contrasting the hoarded wealth (Aw'a) and locked secrets of the heart with the open, retaining ear (Wa'iyah).
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر بين الوعاء الحافظ للحاجة والوعي العقلي، واصفاً الأذن الحافظة للذكرى (واعية)، وجمع الكنز بالأوعية، وسرائر الصدور.
rawText: أَوْعَاهُ الحَدِيثَ 4 He made him to retain the narration in his memory; or to know it, or learn it, by heart. (TA, in art. زكت.) وَعَاهُ 1 He kept it in mind, and considered it. (Msb.) === وَعَى الحَدِيثَ He kept, or retained, the narration in his mind, or memory; or knew it, or learned it, by heart; and studied it until he knew it. (Msb.) === وَعَى, aor. ﹻ, He heeded; paid attention to. وِعَآءٌ A [bag, or wallet, or] receptacle, for travelling-provisions, and for goods or utensils, &c.: (S, MA:) a vessel (?). === وِعَآءُ القَلْبِ [The pericardium]. (TA, voce فُؤَادٌ.) === بَتَثْتُ لَهُ مَا فِى وِعَائِى † I showed him what was in my heart. (Er-Rághib, TA, in art. شكو and شكى.) === حَرْفُ الوِعَآءِ [The adverbial particle فِى]. (IB, in TA, art. وسط.)
